postcss_replacement_contract.tailwindcss_source_none_policy: accepted as migration input and converted to DX-owned @source none scan policy

reference_directive_support.local_css_files: static quoted project-local CSS files are flattened for shared @theme tokens
reference_directive_support.tailwind_or_package_references: diagnosed; no Tailwind runtime or package resolution

source_directive_support.exclude_paths: static local quoted paths through @source not
source_directive_support.inline_safelist: static inline(...) strings with brace and numeric range expansion
source_directive_support.inline_exclusion: static @source not inline(...) strings subtract generated candidates

unsupported_transform_warnings: autoprefixer parity is partial until equal-output browser target tests cover the full property matrix
grid legacy prefixing records warnings instead of synthesizing unsafe -ms-grid placement
full arbitrary PostCSS plugin parity is not claimed by the DX starter replacement contract
oklch/color-mix fallback coverage is intentionally narrow

features.note: Local starter imports flatten from DX-provided source maps.
One-level and recursively parsed starter nesting is expanded.

features.input_css: .card { @media (width >= 48rem) { color: blue; & .title { color: green; } } @supports (display: grid) { display: grid; } }
features.expected_output_css:
@media (min-width: 48rem) { .card { color: blue; } .card .title { color: green; } }
@supports (display: grid) { .card { display: grid; } }
features.note: Nested @media and @supports rules inside selectors are wrapped around the expanded parent selector.
Safe @nest selector rules lower through the same starter nesting path.
Named custom media expands to normalized media queries.
Custom media tokens expand inside compound @media queries without touching declaration values.
Width min/max syntax is normalized for starter CSS.

features.expected_output_css:
@media (min-width: calc(48rem + 0.02px)) { .card { display: block; } }
@media (max-height: calc(40rem - 0.02px)) { .card { display: none; } }
features.note: Strict greater-than and less-than starter media ranges lower to calc-based min/max guards.

features.input_css:
@media (48rem < width <= 72rem) { .card { display: block; } }
@media (30rem <= height < 50rem) { .card { display: none; } }
features.expected_output_css:
@media (min-width: calc(48rem + 0.02px)) and (max-width: 72rem) { .card { display: block; } }
@media (min-height: 30rem) and (max-height: calc(50rem - 0.02px)) { .card { display: none; } }
features.note: Mixed strict/inclusive starter media ranges lower to readable min/max queries.
Safe custom selectors expand through nesting.
Legacy target emits common physical fallbacks; full writing-mode semantics are not claimed.

features.input_css: .logical { margin-inline-start: 1rem; padding-inline-end: 2rem; border-inline-start: 1px solid red; text-align: start; }
features.expected_output_css: .logical { margin-left: 1rem; margin-inline-start: 1rem; padding-right: 2rem; padding-inline-end: 2rem; border-left: 1px solid red; border-inline-start: 1px solid red; text-align: left; text-align: start; }
features.note: Official starters get LTR physical fallbacks for directional logical declarations with receipt warnings.

features.expected_output_css: .button { -webkit-user-select: none; user-select: none; -webkit-appearance: none; appearance: none; }
features.note: Measured canaries emit prefixes, but full Autoprefixer parity remains unclaimed.

features.input_css: .panel { display: inline-flex; hyphens: auto; text-size-adjust: 100%; mask-image: linear-gradient(black, transparent); }
features.note: Legacy target emits additional form, text, mask, transform, and flexbox canary prefixes while avoiding universal parity claims.
High-value color notation fallback is covered narrowly.

features.input_css: .hero { place-items: center start; place-content: space-between center; place-self: stretch end; }
features.expected_output_css: .hero { align-items: center; justify-items: start; place-items: center start; align-content: space-between; justify-content: center; place-content: space-between center; align-self: stretch; justify-self: end; place-self: stretch end; }
features.note: Legacy target emits physical align/justify fallbacks for starter place-* declarations.

features.expected_output_css: .hero { background-image: -webkit-image-set(url("hero.avif") type("image/avif") 1x, url("hero.png") type("image/png") 1x); background-image: image-set(url("hero.avif") type("image/avif") 1x, url("hero.png") type("image/png") 1x); }
features.note: Legacy target emits WebKit image-set() function prefixes for responsive image starter CSS.

features.expected_output_css: .card { color: #1d4ed8; color: var(--brand); padding-top: 1rem; padding-top: env(safe-area-inset-top, 1rem); }
features.note: Official starter CSS emits readable fallbacks for simple root custom properties and env fallback arguments.

features.input_css:
:root { --brand: #1d4ed8; }
.card { color: var(--missing-fg, #111827); border: 1px solid var(--brand); padding: var(--space, calc(1rem + 2px)); }
features.expected_output_css: .card { color: #111827; color: var(--missing-fg, #111827); border: 1px solid #1d4ed8; border: 1px solid var(--brand); padding: calc(1rem + 2px); padding: var(--space, calc(1rem + 2px)); }
features.note: var() fallbacks substitute full declaration values using root tokens or authored fallback arguments.
Safe single-level :is() and :where() selector lists lower for legacy targets; :has() remains diagnostic-only.
Safe single-level :not() selector lists lower to chained :not() selectors for legacy targets.
Simple srgb hex color-mix() values emit readable rgb fallbacks; broader color spaces remain warning-only.
Simple HWB colors with numeric or deg hues emit rgb/rgba fallbacks while preserving the authored color.

features.expected_output_css: .button { background-image: linear-gradient(to right, red, rgba(255, 0, 0, 0)); background-image: linear-gradient(to right, red, transparent); }
features.note: Simple transparent gradient stops emit same-color transparent fallbacks while retaining the authored gradient.

features.expected_output_css: .layout { display: -ms-grid; display: grid; -ms-grid-columns: 12rem 1fr; grid-template-columns: 12rem 1fr; -ms-grid-rows: auto 1fr; grid-template-rows: auto 1fr; }
features.note: Legacy target emits safe -ms-grid display and track canaries but still warns that placement synthesis is not universal.
:has() is preserved with a diagnostic warning for legacy targets.
Unsupported color functions stay readable and receipt-warned.
Transparent gradient risk is warned instead of silently rewritten.

features.expected_output_css: .print { page-break-before: always; break-before: page; page-break-inside: avoid; break-inside: avoid; }
features.note: Print fragmentation fallbacks are emitted for common starter declarations.

features.expected_output_css:
.flex { display: -webkit-box; display: -ms-flexbox; display: flex; }
.grid { display: grid; }
features.note: Flex legacy display prefixes are emitted; grid legacy placement is warning-only.
Receipts expose source-origin and coarse mapping fields for consumers.
Compatibility output remains readable; minification is not enabled here.

unsupported_transform_warnings: autoprefixer parity is partial until equal-output browser target tests cover the full property matrix
grid legacy prefixing records warnings instead of synthesizing unsafe -ms-grid placement
full arbitrary PostCSS plugin parity is not claimed by the DX starter replacement contract
oklch/color-mix fallback coverage is intentionally narrow

tailwind_equal_output_canary_contract.run_policy: checked-in migration/compatibility input fixture only; no package install, server, build, broad suite, or Tailwind execution required

browser_compat_receipt_contract.run_policy: checked-in migration/compatibility input fixture only; no package install, server, build, broad suite, or Tailwind/PostCSS execution required

css_pruning.policy: dx-style emits CSS only for retained static tokens and authored dx classes; unsupported or dynamic class candidates are reported instead of shipping broad CSS.

unsupported_scanned_class_findings.reason: tailwind-like class was scanned but dx-style did not generate CSS for it
unsupported_scanned_class_findings.remediation: Use a supported dx-style utility, add engine support, or move semantic component styling into authored CSS.
